### `r coin_name` {#`r coin_name`}

#### The hierarchy of `r coin_name`{-}

The dendrogram shows how indicators are aggregated up to Level 4 (Vulnerability), with the contribution of each indicator to the next level represented by the colour and thickness of the edges. Contributions are determined by two factors: (1) the assigned weight and (2) the use of the limiting-factor (max-based) approach. Specifically, contribution is calculated as the proportion of units for which an indicator is selected (i.e., number of selections divided by total units), multiplied by its weight.

This visualisation is particularly important under the max-based approach, as the likelihood of an indicator being selected varies substantially with its absolute value, resulting in uneven contributions across indicators.

```{r hierarchy, warning=FALSE, fig.height=15, fig.width=15,  dev='svg', out.width="100%"}

plot_coin_hierarchy(h_df)

```

#### Data processing log of `r coin_name` {-}

- n_minmax: scale using the min and max value of the variable for normalisation, bounded by 0 to 100 (or 1 to 100 for geometric mean) or 0 to 1.

- a_amean: the weighted arithmetic mean for aggregation

- a_gmean: the weighted geometric mean for aggregation

```{r log, echo=FALSE, out.width="90%"}

extract_log_table(coin) %>% 
  kableExtra::kbl(
    escape = FALSE,
    caption = "Extracted data-processing specifications from the coin log. The table summarises the parameters applied at each processing stage and enables comparison across different coins."
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
  ) %>% 
  kableExtra::column_spec(1:4, width = "10em")

```

#### Vulnerability of `r coin_name` {-}

<style type="text/css">
    div.plotly { height: auto !important;}
</style>

```{r CI4, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.height=5, fig.width=10, out.width="100%"}
plot_coin_bar(coin, "Vulnerability", interactive = TRUE)
```

#### The Sub-Indices of `r coin_name` {-}

<style type="text/css">
    div.plotly { height: auto !important;}
</style>

```{r CI3, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.height=15, fig.width=10, out.width="100%"}

## Static plots ------

subindex <- c("Sensitivity", "Exposure", "Pressure")
heights <- c()

plots_static <- Map(function(x) {

  p1 <- plot_coin_bar(coin, x)
  p2 <- plot_coin_bar(coin, x, mode = "children", ncol = 3)

  patchwork::wrap_plots(p1 / p2) +
    patchwork::plot_layout(heights = c(1, 2)) +
    patchwork::plot_annotation(title = paste0(x, ": ", coin_name))

}, subindex)

## Interactive plots ------

plots_interactive <- lapply(subindex, function(x) {

  # ---- parent ----
  p1 <- plot_coin_bar(coin, x, interactive = TRUE)

  # ---- children ----
  p2 <- plot_coin_bar(
    coin,
    x,
    mode = "children",
    ncol = 3,
    interactive = TRUE
  )

  # ---- count children ----
  n_children <- length(na.omit(
    coin$Meta$Ind$iCode[coin$Meta$Ind$Parent == x]
  ))

  # ---- compute rows (since ncol = 3) ----
  n_rows_child <- ceiling(n_children / 3)

  # ---- dynamic heights ----
  h1 <- 1.5                 # parent weight
  h2 <- max(1, n_rows_child)  # children scale

  heights <- c(h1, h2) / (h1 + h2)

  # ---- subplot ----
  plotly::subplot(
    p1, p2,
    nrows = 2,
    margin = 0.1,
    heights = heights
  ) %>%
    plotly::layout(
      title = list(text = paste0(x, ": ", coin_name)),
      height = 700,
      width = 700
    )

})

htmltools::tagList(plots_interactive)

```

#### Vulnerability scores of `r coin_name`{-}

```{r scoretable}

coin$Meta$Unit %>%
  dplyr::left_join(coin$Data$Aggregated, by = "uCode") %>%
  dplyr::select(uName, Vulnerability, all_of(subindex)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, digits = 2)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(Vulnerability)) %>%
    kableExtra::kbl(
    escape = FALSE,
    caption = "Aggregated vulnerability scores and contributing subindices by species, sorted by highest to lowest estimated vulnerability."
  ) %>%
style_kable(style = "scrollable")
  
```
